sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Silvercorp Metals, Hecla Mining, Other Silver Stocks Surge In Tuesday Pre-Market: What's Going On?
feed.summary: Silvercorp Metals, Hecla Mining, First Majestic Silver, and Endeavour Silver all saw their stock prices surge in pre-market trading, driven by a rally in silver prices that pushed spot silver above $95. This increase is attributed to geopolitical uncertainties, including President Trump's Greenland push, and surging demand for silver in AI and solar technologies. Economist Peter Schiff, however, warns that this metals rally could be a sign of deeper financial troubles, comparing it to the 2007 subprime mortgage crisis.

feed.title: TVA Rebates 10,000 Heat Pump Water Heaters in Q4 of 2025, Showing a Wildly Successful Model
feed.summary: The Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) successfully rebated over 10,000 heat pump water heaters (HPWHs) in Q4 2025, significantly exceeding expectations through a "midstream" incentive model. After initial struggles with upstream and downstream models, TVA's pivot to directly incentivizing distributors and retailers with a $1,300 discount and simplified processes led to remarkable growth and market shift. This success demonstrates the effectiveness of strong rebates and smart program design in accelerating HPWH adoption.

feed.title: Dynasty Wealth Management LLC Buys 9,812 Shares of Marathon Petroleum Corporation $MPC
feed.summary: Dynasty Wealth Management LLC increased its stake in Marathon Petroleum Corporation (NYSE:MPC) by 134.8% during the third quarter, purchasing an additional 9,812 shares. Other institutional investors also adjusted their holdings in MPC, which currently trades with a market cap of $52.76 billion and has a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating from analysts. The company recently increased its quarterly dividend and reported Q3 earnings that beat analyst estimates.

feed.title: AIG commits up to $3.5bn in strategic partnership with CVC
feed.summary: American International Group (AIG) has formed a strategic partnership with CVC, committing up to $3.5 billion across credit strategies and private equity secondaries. This collaboration includes AIG serving as a cornerstone investor in CVC's new private equity secondaries evergreen platform, contributing up to $1.5 billion from its existing portfolio. Additionally, AIG plans to allocate up to $2 billion to CVC-managed separately managed accounts and funds, with an initial $1 billion deployment expected by 2026 for diversified private and liquid credit strategies.

feed.title: TCV and Blackstone back Pennylane at $4.25bn valuation
feed.summary: TCV and Blackstone have co-led a €175 million funding round in Pennylane, a French accounting software company, valuing it at approximately $4.25 billion. This investment reinforces Pennylane's position as a valuable European startup and will fuel its research and development, including AI-powered assistants, and expansion into markets like Germany. The deal highlights continued investor interest in strong European software platforms despite a selective venture funding environment.

feed.title: Earnings Scheduled For January 20, 2026
feed.summary: This article lists companies scheduled to report earnings on January 20, 2026, including their projected earnings per share and revenue. It categorizes companies based on whether they report before or after the bell, featuring significant companies like D.R. Horton, Fastenal, and Netflix.

feed.title: Morgan Stanley reiterates Overweight rating on Eli Lilly stock ahead of obesity trial data By Investing.com
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has reaffirmed its Overweight rating and $1,290.00 price target for Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY) as the company awaits results from its REDEFINE-4 Phase 3 obesity trial. The trial compares Eli Lilly's Zepbound with Novo Nordisk's CagriSema, and Morgan Stanley outlined three potential outcomes, with a bull case for Eli Lilly if CagriSema shows inferior efficacy or tolerability. This comes amidst other strategic moves by Eli Lilly, including a potential acquisition and a delayed FDA decision on another weight-loss medication.

feed.title: BHP (NYSE: BHP) raises Jansen Stage 1 capex to US$8.4B and eyes 4.15 Mtpa
feed.summary: BHP has revised the cost and schedule for its Jansen Stage 1 potash project, increasing the total investment estimate to US$8.4 billion and pushing first production back to mid-CY2027. Despite the higher cost, the project is 75% complete and still expected to deliver 4.15 Mtpa of potash, with strong underlying EBITDA margins projected. BHP has implemented a response plan to address cost and schedule risks and is applying these lessons to Jansen Stage 2.

feed.title: If You'd Invested $1,000 in Costco Stock 20 Years Ago, Here's How Much You'd Have Today
feed.summary: Costco's consistent business model, featuring low prices and bulk packaging, has attracted millions of members and led to strong financial performance over decades. An investment of $1,000 in Costco stock 20 years ago, including dividends, would now be worth $27,600, significantly outperforming the S&P 500. Despite a slight dip in 2025, the company continues to expand and is expected to reward investors in the future.

feed.title: CDW (CDW) Faces Downgrade and Price Target Cut by Morgan Stanley | CDW Stock News
feed.summary: Morgan Stanley has downgraded CDW (CDW) from 'Overweight' to 'Equal-Weight' and reduced its price target from $177.00 to $141.00. This adjustment is based on a broader evaluation of CDW's market position and financial outlook, highlighting the dynamic nature of analyst evaluations for the IT solutions provider. Despite the downgrade, the average target price from 11 analysts suggests a potential upside of 38.42% from the current price.

feed.title: Kendra Scott, Yeti, Valero and other Texas companies sue U.S. over tariffs
feed.summary: Over a dozen Texas companies, including Yeti, Kendra Scott, and Valero Energy Corp.'s marketing arm, are suing the U.S. government over tariffs imposed during the Trump administration, challenging the president's use of executive action in global trade. Despite their lawsuits, most companies are reluctant to speak publicly due to fears of retaliation from the White House. Experts note that the widespread legal challenges highlight how the trade war has disrupted various economic sectors and caused significant uncertainty for businesses across the state.

feed.title: Assessing Sandisk (SNDK) Valuation After A Sharp Multi‑Month Share Price Surge
feed.summary: Sandisk (SNDK) has experienced a significant stock price surge, leading to a closer examination of its valuation. Despite the impressive gains, Sandisk's Price-to-Sales ratio of 7.8x is considerably higher than its US tech peers and the broader industry average, indicating it might be overvalued. While a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) model suggests a fair value close to its current price, investors should consider the premium they are paying for future growth potential as the company currently reports a net loss.

feed.title: China steers the Gulf’s driverless future as U.S. rivals stay home
feed.summary: Chinese autonomous vehicle companies like WeRide, Baidu, and Pony.ai are rapidly expanding their robotaxi operations across the Gulf region, securing exclusive deals and forming partnerships with local governments. This contrasts sharply with U.S. rivals such as Waymo and Cruise, which remain largely focused on domestic markets due to regulatory hurdles and safety incidents. The Gulf nations see autonomous vehicles as key to their Vision 2030 goals and find Chinese firms appealing due to their data sovereignty guarantees, flexible financing, and willingness to localize operations.

feed.title: Moderna, Merck Show Long-Term Survival For mRNA-Keytruda Combo
feed.summary: Moderna and Merck announced that their mRNA neoantigen therapy, intismeran autogene, combined with Keytruda, reduced the risk of recurrence or death in advanced melanoma patients by nearly half at the five-year mark of a mid-stage trial. This data reinforces previous readouts and supports the companies' ongoing efforts in a fully enrolled Phase III trial for melanoma and other cancers. The therapy aims to boost the immune system's efficacy against cancer by generating targeted T cell responses.

feed.title: The Past Five Years for Align Technology (NASDAQ:ALGN) Investors Has Not Been Profitabl
feed.summary: Align Technology (NASDAQ:ALGN) shareholders have experienced a significant loss of 68% over the past five years, despite a recent 26% share price increase in the last quarter. This poor performance is linked to a 25% annual drop in earnings per share over the same period. While insiders have been buying shares, the company's long-term profitability remains a concern for investors.

feed.title: A new Royal Caribbean cruise terminal for big ships is coming to PortMiami
feed.summary: Royal Caribbean is building a new $345 million cruise terminal at PortMiami, expected to be completed in late 2027. This new terminal will be capable of servicing massive cruise ships, accommodating up to 7,000 passengers. The groundbreaking for the terminal took place earlier this month, following the arrival of the Icon of the Seas in Miami.

feed.title: Moderna & Merck Announce 5-Year Data for Intismeran Autogene in Combination With KEYTRUDA(R) (pembrolizumab) Demonstrated Sustained Improvement in the Primary Endpoint of Recurrence-Free Survival in Patients With High-Risk Stage III/IV Melanoma Following Complete Resection
feed.summary: Moderna and Merck announced positive five-year follow-up data from their Phase 2b KEYNOTE-942/mRNA-4157-P201 study, revealing that intismeran autogene in combination with KEYTRUDA significantly reduced the risk of recurrence or death by 49% in patients with high-risk Stage III/IV melanoma after complete resection. This sustained improvement in recurrence-free survival supports further clinical development, with eight Phase 2 and Phase 3 trials currently underway for various cancer types. The companies plan to present additional detailed data from this study at a future medical conference.

feed.title: Memphis gets $500K from TVA for energy-efficient home repairs
feed.summary: The City of Memphis is set to receive $500,000 from the Tennessee Valley Authority (TVA) to fund energy-efficient home repairs. This new funding will enhance the Residential Repair and Rehabilitation Assistance Program, specifically targeting residents previously ineligible for such aid due to existing home conditions. Additionally, the article covers leadership changes at ALSAC, new EPA turbine regulations, and local environmental advocacy efforts.

feed.title: West Synchrony™ S1 Prefillable Syringe System Commercially Available at Pharmapack
feed.summary: West Pharmaceutical Services, Inc. announced the global commercial availability of its West Synchrony™ S1 prefillable syringe (PFS) system at Pharmapack 2026 in Paris, France. This system offers fully integrated and verified prefillable syringe solutions for various biologics and vaccines, supporting reliable and efficient drug delivery. West will also showcase its expertise through learning labs at the event, addressing industry trends like the growing demand for combination products and the shift from hospital to home care.

feed.title: New prefilled syringe system targets biologic and vaccine delivery
feed.summary: West Pharmaceutical Services (NYSE: WST) has announced the global commercial availability of its West Synchrony™ S1 prefillable syringe (PFS) system at Pharmapack 2026. This system offers various configurations for biologics and vaccines, including different syringe sizes, needle options, and plunger technologies. The company will also host learning labs and a product gallery at Pharmapack to showcase its drug delivery solutions and approaches to combination products.

feed.title: AI now filters financial advisors’ leads in Prudential’s retail arm
feed.summary: Prudential Advisors, the retail arm of Prudential Financial (NYSE: PRU), has significantly enhanced its Advisor Leads program by integrating AI and data science. These upgrades include Gen AI-derived customer insights for personalized outreach, lead propensity modeling to prioritize high-conversion prospects, and the integration of hundreds of third-party attributes. This initiative aims to improve lead quality, increase conversion rates, and enable advisors to focus on more meaningful client interactions.

feed.title: Prudential Advisors Enhances Advisor Leads Program with AI and Data Science
feed.summary: Prudential Advisors has significantly enhanced its Advisor Leads program by integrating AI and data science across the entire lead lifecycle. These advancements, embedded in the Prudential Advisors Connect platform, include Gen AI-derived customer insights for personalized outreach and lead propensity modeling to prioritize high-conversion opportunities. The program aims to improve lead quality, increase conversion rates, and enable advisors to focus on providing relevant advice more efficiently, supported by enhanced lead insights from additional third-party data.

feed.title: SBIT Stock Fund Price and Chart — AMEX:SBIT
feed.summary: The ProShares UltraShort Bitcoin ETF (SBIT) is a bearish, leveraged ETF designed for short-term trading, aiming to provide double the inverse daily returns of its underlying Bloomberg Bitcoin Index. It primarily invests in swap agreements, and its returns can vary significantly if held for longer than a day due to its daily reset mechanism. Key statistics show an AUM of $105.73 million, a 0.95% expense ratio, and year-to-date returns of -14.91%.

feed.title: (AGNC) Movement as an Input in Quant Signal Sets

It provides specific trading strategies including long, breakout, and short positions with entry zones, targets, and stop losses. The analysis also covers multi-timeframe signal strength, support, and resistance levels.

feed.title: GSK To Acquire RAPT Therapeutics In $2.2 Billion Bet On Food Allergy Breakthrough
feed.summary: GSK has agreed to acquire RAPT Therapeutics for $2.2 billion, primarily for its lead drug ozureprubart, a Phase 2b anti-IgE antibody for food allergies. This acquisition strengthens GSK's allergy pipeline with a potential best-in-class treatment offering less frequent dosing. Concurrently, Pfizer is exiting its 11.7% stake in ViiV Healthcare, with Shionogi increasing its holding.
